task: I’m putting together a very simple reading-support mini lesson for a student who does better with easier-to-scan text, and I want you to help me pull the pieces together in the browser. Start on Google and find one online English grammar practice quiz that feels appropriate for about 5th grade, then open the actual quiz page so you can verify it’s really a student-facing practice activity and leave that tab open for me as a reference; I need the quiz title and direct URL. Then use Google again to find a printable worksheet or practice page for an 8th-grade student about basic marketing strategies like product, price, place, and promotion or closely related introductory marketing concepts, because I want it to work as an extension activity after the grammar warm-up; open the real worksheet or resource page in its own tab so I can see that it looks classroom-appropriate and printable, and note the title and URL. After that, go to Slidesgo and pick a fun, classroom-appropriate presentation template that could reasonably hold both the grammar warm-up and the marketing extension in one student lesson deck, and open the template’s actual page so I can see the preview images; please include the template name, URL, and whether it’s available for Google Slides or PowerPoint. Finally, go to 10015.io’s bionic reading converter and convert this exact lesson intro into bionic reading format so I can paste it into the first slide of the template you chose: “Today we will warm up with a short grammar quiz, then practice how people use basic marketing strategies like product, price, place, and promotion. Read each direction carefully and do your best.” Please keep the useful tabs open and send me the quiz, worksheet, template, and the converted text in one clean summary.
grader_reasoning:
R1: success - The agent successfully searched for a 5th-grade English grammar practice quiz using Google (Step 4). It identified and opened a relevant quiz page on ProProfs (Step 5, 7, 8). The quiz is titled "5th Grade English Quiz: Test Your Grammar Skills" and is located at https://www.proprofs.com/quiz-school/story.php?title=mjgyoda4naugdk. The agent verified the content of the page (Step 8) and included the title and URL in its final summary (Step 41). The tab remained open throughout the process.

R2: success - The agent successfully searched for an 8th-grade marketing worksheet using Google (Step 13). It identified and opened a PDF resource from Biz Kids titled "The Marketing Mix - Biz Kids" (Step 14, 22). The resource is a printable PDF lesson plan/worksheet that covers the 4 Ps (product, price, place, and promotion) and is appropriate for the target grade level (Grades 6-9). The agent included the title and URL in its final summary (Step 41).

R3: success - The agent successfully navigated to Slidesgo, searched for "fun classroom" templates, and selected the "Crayons Theme for Classroom Presentation" (https://slidesgo.com/theme/crayons-theme-for-classroom). The agent verified the template's details, including the preview images and the availability of download options for Google Slides, PowerPoint, and Canva. The final response includes the template name, the URL, and the download options as required.

R4: success - The agent successfully navigated to 10015.io, input the specified lesson intro text into the Bionic Reading Converter, and performed the conversion. The agent then extracted the converted text from the page's DOM (as shown in step 37) and included the correctly formatted bionic text in its final summary (step 41). The output matches the requirement to provide the converted text.
